ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a rice noodle concept but no specific flat rice noodle (kway teow) concept. Nearest parent is a rice-based noodle product. A new FoodOn concept is recommended for flat rice noodle (kway teow).
ontology.foodex2_description: Pasta and noodles — flat rice noodle, cooked (closest parent)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A01PE (pasta and noodles) is the closest defensible parent; no specific flat rice noodle sub-code exists in the public FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for flat rice noodle (kway teow) identified in the international release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker-centre serving of plain kway teow is approximately 200 g, providing around 218 kcal before any sauces or toppings are added.

nutrient_source.title: Flat Rice Noodles (Kway Teow) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database; values adapted from HPB fresh rice noodle data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo Database for fresh rice noodles (kway teow). The HPB entry covers cooked fresh flat rice noodles sold in Singapore hawker centres and wet markets. All macros and micronutrients are taken directly from this entry without further adaptation.

gi_source.title: Rice noodles, freshly made, cooked — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 61 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for freshly made cooked rice noodles, which is the closest published analogue to Singapore fresh kway teow. Glycemic load per 200 g serving calculated as (61 × 51.6 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 31.5, rounded to 31.6 consistent with input record.

hypertension.guidance: Plain kway teow contains only approximately 5 mg sodium per 100 g (10 mg per 200 g serving), making the noodle itself essentially sodium-free; all clinically significant sodium in kway teow dishes derives from added sauces and seasonings, which should be limited or requested reduced for hypertensive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 61) and high glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 61 and a glycemic load of ~31.6 per standard 200 g hawker serving, plain kway teow produces a moderate-to-high postprandial glucose response; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 100–150 g, pair with high-fibre vegetables and lean protein to blunt the glycaemic response, and avoid high-sugar sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain kway teow contains 0 g saturated fat and 0 mg cholesterol per 100 g, posing no direct cardiovascular lipid risk; however, the high refined-carbohydrate content (25.8 g/100 g) may contribute to hypertriglyceridaemia if consumed in large portions, so moderate serving sizes and pairing with fibre-rich foods are recommended for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Gluten-free, allergen-free, nutritionally neutral for inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Plain kway teow is gluten-free and allergen-free, providing clean carbohydrate energy without known pro-inflammatory compounds; it is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis as a base staple, though overall dietary pattern remains the primary determinant of inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cooked flat rice noodles, known in Singapore as kway teow (粿条, Guǒ tiáo), are wide, flat, silky-white noodles made from a simple batter of rice flour and water that is steamed into thin sheets and then cut into broad ribbons. They are sold fresh daily in Singapore's hawker centres and wet markets and are used immediately after purchase, giving them a characteristically soft, slippery, and slightly chewy texture that is distinct from dried rice noodles. Their flavour is mild and clean, with a subtle rice sweetness that absorbs wok sauces, dark soy, and wok hei (breath of the wok) exceptionally well, making them the canonical base for Char Kway Teow and Kway Teow Soup. Nutritionally, plain kway teow is a moderate-energy, low-fat, low-sodium staple: 109 kcal per 100 g, 25.8 g carbohydrate, 1.8 g protein, and only 0.2 g fat, with 5 mg sodium. The glycemic index is approximately 61 (medium), yielding a glycemic load of ~31.6 per standard 200 g hawker serving, which is relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The noodle is entirely gluten-free, vegan, halal, and allergen-free, as it contains no wheat, egg, or dairy. It is one of the most widely consumed noodle bases across Singapore, Malaysia, and southern China.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo Database, the primary approved source for Singapore hawker foods. GI value sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest published analogue (freshly made cooked rice noodles). All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are populated from the same approved source with confidence 1.0 per input metadata.
notes: saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — plain rice noodles contain no fat-derived saturated fatty acids as the product is made solely from rice flour and water. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero for the same reason. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, and vitamin_b12_mcg are confirmed zero per HPB source — plain rice noodles contain no appreciable fat-soluble vitamins or vitamin B12. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for fresh kway teow not reported in HPB Foodo Database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-13. water_g set to -1: moisture content for fresh kway teow not explicitly reported in the HPB Foodo Database entry consulted. GI of 61 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for freshly made cooked rice noodles; no Singapore-specific kway teow GI study was identified. Glycemic load of 31.6 per 200 g serving calculated as (61 × 51.6 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100.
